description: ProtocolImpl/ProtocolDispatchArm (compiler2/protocol.rs) key callbacks as HashMap<(String, usize), ProtocolCallbackImpl> — the last name-shaped callable keying in compiler2. resolve_protocol_call (jobs/semantic.rs) already holds the callback's FunctionId at the callsite and does function_ref.name.clone() per arm per resolution just to perform the lookup. Impl registration knows which protocol callback each impl function implements, so the maps can key by the callback FunctionId directly: HashMap<FunctionId, ProtocolCallbackImpl>. Deletes the parallel name surface (the per-protocol callback interface is then represented once, by the protocol module's interface) and the per-arm String clones. This is the 'protocol-specific name specialness that exists only because callables were not first-class' named by epic fz-rh2.17 — remove, don't paper over. Verify: protocol drive tests (00272/00006 fixtures et al), compiler2:: green, full-suite delta empty.
title: Compiler2 protocols: key impl callbacks by callback FunctionId, not (name, arity)
